confirmed.title: Anti-patterns section recommends TenantManager::runFor(null, ...) but runFor requires a non-nullable Model
confirmed.evidence: SKILL line 84 tells consumers to do admin overrides with `TenantManager::runFor(null, fn () => ...)`. Source packages/tenant/src/TenantManager.php:108 declares `public function runFor(Model $tenant, Closure $callback): mixed` — the first parameter is a non-nullable `Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model`. Passing `null` throws a TypeError; the null-drop behavior lives in the separate `forget()` method (TenantManager.php:129), not in runFor.
confirmed.fix: Change the documented call to pass a concrete tenant model (e.g. `TenantManager::runFor($tenant, fn () => ...)`), or point the 'admin override' guidance at `forget()` / `withoutGlobalScope(TenantScope::class)` since runFor cannot accept null.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. packages/tenant/SKILL.md:84 tells consumers to do the "admin override" via `TenantManager::runFor(null, fn () => ...)`. But packages/tenant/src/TenantManager.php:108 declares `public function runFor(Model $tenant, Closure $callback): mixed` — the first parameter is a non-nullable `Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model` (imported at line 14). Passing `null` throws a TypeError at runtime; `runFor` never nulls the tenant, it sets it to `$tenant` (line 113). The null-accepting/null-dropping behavior lives in `set(?Model $tenant)` (line 81, "Passing null is equivalent to forget()") and `forget()` (line 129), not in `runFor`. So the documented "admin override" recipe is broken code. This is in the tenant package (a prioritized previously-unswept package) and belongs to no SWEPT family — it is not a reference/php signature file, not a version-line mismatch, not a trilingual mirror. Proposed fix stands: pass a concrete tenant to runFor, or point the admin-override guidance at forget()/withoutGlobalScope. File: /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/tenant/SKILL.md line 84; source /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/tenant/src/TenantManager.php line 108.

confirmed.title: ExportAction factory documented as make(string $name = 'export') but source make() has no default
confirmed.evidence: SKILL line 17 states `Factory \`make(string $name = 'export')\``. Source packages/export/src/Actions/ExportAction.php:61 declares `public static function make(string $name): static` with no default value, so `ExportAction::make()` (no argument) is a fatal ArgumentCountError; a name must always be passed.
confirmed.fix: Update the SKILL to `make(string $name): static` (name is required, no default), matching ExportAction.php:61.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. packages/export/src/Actions/ExportAction.php:61 declares `public static function make(string $name): static` with NO default value, so `ExportAction::make()` with no argument throws a fatal ArgumentCountError — the name is mandatory. packages/export/SKILL.md:17 documents the factory as `make(string $name = 'export')`, which falsely implies the argument is optional. This is a genuine doc-vs-code contradiction. It is not covered by any SWEPT family: the R17 export sweep concerned export dependencies (composer require), and the SWEPT signature family targets apps/docs/reference/php/*.md, not packages/export/SKILL.md. Proposed fix (change SKILL to `make(string $name): static`, name required) is correct and actionable.

verdict.reason:
CONFIRMED and NEW. apps/docs/es/SKILL.md:22 reads "**Por chegar (DOCS-002..008):**" and lists all seven deliverables (getting-started, concept guides, blog tutorial, PHP reference, TS reference, Filament/Nova migration guides, MCP docs) as not-yet-done. I verified on disk that every one of these is shipped under apps/docs/: guide/getting-started.md; guide/{panels,resources,fields,tables-forms,actions,auth}.md; examples/blog-admin.md; reference/php/*.md (7 files); reference/ts/*.md (fields,hooks,react,types,ui); guide/migration/{from-filament,from-nova}.md; guide/mcp-server.md + advanced/mcp.md. So the "Por chegar" status genuinely contradicts the shipped state.

Not a duplicate of any SWEPT family: the ledger sweeps cover reference/php/*.md method signatures, README Artisan lists, and trilingual guide/migration/reference *content* drift — none touch the SKILL.md "Status / Por chegar" ticket-status block. Notably, the finding's own premise that this "mirrors the corrected pt-BR/SKILL.md" is inaccurate: apps/docs/pt-BR/SKILL.md:22 STILL says "Por chegar (DOCS-002..008)" and was never corrected, so this is a live, untouched, distinct file+line contradiction.
